LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT EDWARD GRAMMENS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. CROSS-DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PRODUCTION. GRAMMENS HAS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT NEWMARK PACIFIC IS UNABLE TO PROVE EACH OF ITS CLAIMS. THERE ALSO ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO WHETHER THE JUNE 8 AGREEMENT WAS PROCURED BY FRAUD, AS TO MS. FINIGAN'S AUTHORITY TO SIGN THE AGREEMENT, AS TO THE KNOWLEDGE OF NEWMARK PACIFIC SO AS TO MAKE EFFECTIVE THE RELEASE IN THE JUNE 8 LETTER AND THE NEED FOR GRAMMENS TO DISCLOSE THE DETAILS OF THE KMI TRANSACTION. Off Calendar Jul-30-2008 Text Ruling

Plaintiff Edward E. Grammens' Application To Quash Or Modify Deposition Subpoena And For A Protective Order
Off Calendar Jul-18-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Terry J. Mollica, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Edward E. Grammens
Diane L. Webb, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Newmark Pacific, Inc.

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. Plaintiff Edward E. Grammens' motion to quash or modify deposition subpoenas and for a protective order is granted in part and denied in part. The motion to modify the subpoena is granted. Category #6 is stricken. The motion for a protective order to limit third party depositions is denied.

The Court has completed the in camera review of specified documents on plaintiff's privilege log pursuant to the motion to compel production on June 12, 2008 and is prepared to order production of the following documents in un-redacted form: 000222, 000223, 000230, log #39, 41. The Court is prepared to order production of the following documents in redacted form: 000133-000138, 000160, 000172, 000186, 000225, log# 65, 66, 68, 72, 75, 83. Commissioner: Bruce Chan. Not reported.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-17-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS DEFENDANT EDWARD GRAMMENS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Appearances:
Gregg Yates of Morgan Lewis (442-1365) for cross complainant and responding party Newmark Pacific, Inc.
Terry Mollica of Chiarelli & Mollica (925-974-3325) for cross defendant and moving party Edward Grammens

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied. Cross-defendant has failed to shift the burden of production. Grammens has failed to demonstrate that Newmark Pacific is unable to prove each of its claims. There also are triable issues of fact as to whether the June 8 agreement was procured by fraud, as to Ms. Finigan's authority to sign the agreement, as to the knowledge of Newmark Pacific so as to make effective the release in the June 8 letter and the need for Grammens to disclose the details of the KMI transaction. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

MINI MINUTES: CROSS DEFENDANT EDWARD GRAMMENS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Appearances:
Terry Mollica of Chiarelli & Mollica (925-974-3325) for cross defendant and moving party
Greg Yates and Diane Webb of Morgan Lewis (442-1365) for cross complainant and responding party Newmark Pacific

Argued and the Court modified its ruling as follows: the matter is continued to July 17, 2008. Parties are to submit further briefing (no longer than 5 pages) by 5:00 pm on July 15th, that crystallizes the core facts that each party believes supports their positions as to the enforceability of the termination agreement. Parties are to cite the following: 1- either undisputed or disputed facts in the separate statement; and 2- evidence that supports it. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick Mahoney; Reporter: Kimberly Jennings, CSR 11452

Notice Of Motion To Quash Or Modify Deposition Subpoena And For A Protective Order Or, Alternatively, For An Order Shortening Time On Hearing On Motion To Quash And For A Protective Order
Off Calendar Mar-19-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCE:
Terry J. Mollica, Esq., for plaintiff and moving party: Edward Grammens
Gregg Paris Yates, Esq., for defendant and opposing party: Newmark Pacific, Inc. dba Newmark Knight Frank

AFTER HEARING, TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED WITH MODIFICATION. Plaintiff Edward Grammens' motion to quash or modify deposition subpoena to third party and for a protective order is granted in part and denied in part. As to the motion for a protective order, the parties are ordered to meet and confer to draft a proposed order that while limiting the use of any documents to purposes related to the litigation, does not require disclosure of the identity of any persons who consults with counsel. Any person to whom materials are disclosed that have been designated as confidential shall be advised that further dissemination of the information constitutes a violation of a court order.
MODIFICATION: Subpoena to third party modified to categories 2, 3, 4 to include limitation of KMI/CVT transaction.
COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN. Not reported.
